revision.text:
{{Short description|Kofun period burial mound in Asakura, Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Oda Chausutsuka Kofun | native_name =小田茶臼塚古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = | image_size = | alt = | caption = | map_type = Japan Fukuoka Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Oda Chausutsuka Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|33|23|18|N|130|39|53|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Asakura, Fukuoka]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Kyushu]] | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.5th century | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes ={{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|33|23|18|N|130|39|53|E}}}}}}
The {{nihongo|'''Oda Chausutsuka Kofun'''|小田茶臼塚古墳}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[zenpokoenfun|burial mound]], located in the Oda neighborhood of former [[Amagi, Fukuoka|Amagi]] city in what is now part of the city of [[Asakura, Fukuoka|Asakura]], [[Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1979.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/db/heritages/detail/202896|title=小田茶臼塚古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=December 20, 2023}}</ref> It is believed to have been built in the late 5th century.
==Overview== The Oda Chausutsuka Kofun is located on the eastern edge of the Chikugo Plain, on the right bank of the Sada River, a tributary of the [[Chikugo River]]. Its existence has been known for a long time, and it is listed in a local history texts on [[Chikuzen Province]] in 1798 and 1863. The total length of the tumulus is 54.5 meters, and it is a {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. The diameter of the posterior circular portion is 39.7 meters, and the width of the rectangular anterior portion is 25 meters, narrowing to 14.5 meter at the construction between the two portions. The length of this anterior portion is only 14.5 meters, which is short for a typical tumulus of this style. The height of the tumulus os 5 meters. The tumulus is orientated to the north, but the opening for the horizontal passage-style stone [[burial chamber]] is to the southwest. This chamber measures 3.5 meters long by 2.2 meters wide and 1.6 meters high. The tumulus was surrounded by a moat 4 meters wide and 0.6 meters deep. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The posterior circular part was constructed in three stages, with the bottom stage being carved out of the ground, and the second and higher stages being mounds with ''[[fukiishi]]''. The anterior part was built in two stages, and in the second stage a row of cylindrical and "morning-glory shaped" ''[[haniwa]]'' was detected. Additionally, a large [[Sue ware]] jar and a utensil stand were placed above the constriction.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
During the construction of a road in 1928, the southeastern portion of the posterior circular part was destroyed and the burial chamber exposed. A local teacher from Asakura Junior High School (currently Asakura High School) conducted a survey, and many of the excavated [[grave goods]] are still stored at the school. A formal [[archaeological excavation]] was not conducted until 1973. The grave goods recovered in 1928 included a short armor with horizontal strips, a helmet, a [[pauldron]], an iron sword, two iron spears, five iron bars, a bundle of iron arrowheads, eight cyliindrical beads, 226 round glass beads, and 161 small beads. Also found were horse harnesses, a four-ringed bell, stone pestle and ancient Sue ware pottery.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
Approximately three kilometers north of the tumulus is the Amagi Historical Museum, which displays excavated items. The tumulus itself is approximately eight minutes by car from [[Amagi Station]] on the [[Amagi Railway Amagi Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Fukuoka)]]
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== *[https://www.city.asakura.lg.jp/www/contents/1297732321710/index.html Asakura city home page] {{in lang|ja}}

The total length of the tumulus is 54.5 meters, and it is a {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. The diameter of the posterior circular portion is 39.7 meters, and the width of the rectangular anterior portion is 25 meters, narrowing to 14.5 meter at the construction between the two portions. The length of this anterior portion is only 14.5 meters, which is short for a typical tumulus of this style. The height of the tumulus os 5 meters. The tumulus is orientated to the north, but the opening for the horizontal passage-style stone [[burial chamber]] is to the southwest. This chamber measures 3.5 meters long by 2.2 meters wide and 1.6 meters high. The tumulus was surrounded by a moat 4 meters wide and 0.6 meters deep. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The posterior circular part was constructed in three stages, with the bottom stage being carved out of the ground, and the second and higher stages being mounds with ''[[fukiishi]]''. The anterior part was built in two stages, and in the second stage a row of cylindrical and "morning-glory shaped" ''[[haniwa]]'' was detected. Additionally, a large [[Sue ware]] jar and a utensil stand were placed above the constriction.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
During the construction of a road in 1928, the southeastern portion of the posterior circular part was destroyed and the burial chamber exposed. A local teacher from Asakura Junior High School (currently Asakura High School) conducted a survey, and many of the excavated [[grave goods]] are still stored at the school. A formal [[archaeological excavation]] was not conducted until 1973. The grave goods recovered in 1928 included a short armor with horizontal strips, a helmet, a [[pauldron]], an iron sword, two iron spears, five iron bars, a bundle of iron arrowheads, eight cyliindrical beads, 226 round glass beads, and 161 small beads. Also found were horse harnesses, a four-ringed bell, stone pestle and ancient Sue ware pottery.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
Approximately three kilometers north of the tumulus is the Amagi Historical Museum, which displays excavated items. The tumulus itself is approximately eight minutes by car from [[Amagi Station]] on the [[Amagi Railway Amagi Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Fukuoka)]]
==References== {{reflist}}

The anterior part was built in two stages, and in the second stage a row of cylindrical and "morning-glory shaped" ''[[haniwa]]'' was detected. Additionally, a large [[Sue ware]] jar and a utensil stand were placed above the constriction.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
During the construction of a road in 1928, the southeastern portion of the posterior circular part was destroyed and the burial chamber exposed. A local teacher from Asakura Junior High School (currently Asakura High School) conducted a survey, and many of the excavated [[grave goods]] are still stored at the school. A formal [[archaeological excavation]] was not conducted until 1973. The grave goods recovered in 1928 included a short armor with horizontal strips, a helmet, a [[pauldron]], an iron sword, two iron spears, five iron bars, a bundle of iron arrowheads, eight cyliindrical beads, 226 round glass beads, and 161 small beads. Also found were horse harnesses, a four-ringed bell, stone pestle and ancient Sue ware pottery.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
Approximately three kilometers north of the tumulus is the Amagi Historical Museum, which displays excavated items. The tumulus itself is approximately eight minutes by car from [[Amagi Station]] on the [[Amagi Railway Amagi Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>

The {{nihongo|'''Oda Chausutsuka Kofun'''|小田茶臼塚古墳}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[zenpokoenfun|burial mound]], located in the Oda neighborhood of former [[Amagi, Fukuoka|Amagi]] city in what is now part of the city of [[Asakura, Fukuoka|Asakura]], [[Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1979.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/db/heritages/detail/202896|title=小田茶臼塚古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=December 20, 2023}}</ref> It is believed to have been built in the late 5th century.
==Overview== The Oda Chausutsuka Kofun is located on the eastern edge of the Chikugo Plain, on the right bank of the Sada River, a tributary of the [[Chikugo River]]. Its existence has been known for a long time, and it is listed in a local history texts on [[Chikuzen Province]] in 1798 and 1863. The total length of the tumulus is 54.5 meters, and it is a {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. The diameter of the posterior circular portion is 39.7 meters, and the width of the rectangular anterior portion is 25 meters, narrowing to 14.5 meter at the construction between the two portions. The length of this anterior portion is only 14.5 meters, which is short for a typical tumulus of this style. The height of the tumulus os 5 meters. The tumulus is orientated to the north, but the opening for the horizontal passage-style stone [[burial chamber]] is to the southwest. This chamber measures 3.5 meters long by 2.2 meters wide and 1.6 meters high. The tumulus was surrounded by a moat 4 meters wide and 0.6 meters deep. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>

The anterior part was built in two stages, and in the second stage a row of cylindrical and "morning-glory shaped" ''[[haniwa]]'' was detected. Additionally, a large [[Sue ware]] jar and a utensil stand were placed above the constriction.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
During the construction of a road in 1928, the southeastern portion of the posterior circular part was destroyed and the burial chamber exposed. A local teacher from Asakura Junior High School (currently Asakura High School) conducted a survey, and many of the excavated [[grave goods]] are still stored at the school. A formal [[archaeological excavation]] was not conducted until 1973. The grave goods recovered in 1928 included a short armor with horizontal strips, a helmet, a [[pauldron]], an iron sword, two iron spears, five iron bars, a bundle of iron arrowheads, eight cyliindrical beads, 226 round glass beads, and 161 small beads. Also found were horse harnesses, a four-ringed bell, stone pestle and ancient Sue ware pottery.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
Approximately three kilometers north of the tumulus is the Amagi Historical Museum, which displays excavated items. The tumulus itself is approximately eight minutes by car from [[Amagi Station]] on the [[Amagi Railway Amagi Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>

The anterior part was built in two stages, and in the second stage a row of cylindrical and "morning-glory shaped" ''[[haniwa]]'' was detected. Additionally, a large [[Sue ware]] jar and a utensil stand were placed above the constriction.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
During the construction of a road in 1928, the southeastern portion of the posterior circular part was destroyed and the burial chamber exposed. A local teacher from Asakura Junior High School (currently Asakura High School) conducted a survey, and many of the excavated [[grave goods]] are still stored at the school. A formal [[archaeological excavation]] was not conducted until 1973. The grave goods recovered in 1928 included a short armor with horizontal strips, a helmet, a [[pauldron]], an iron sword, two iron spears, five iron bars, a bundle of iron arrowheads, eight cyliindrical beads, 226 round glass beads, and 161 small beads. Also found were horse harnesses, a four-ringed bell, stone pestle and ancient Sue ware pottery.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
Approximately three kilometers north of the tumulus is the Amagi Historical Museum, which displays excavated items. The tumulus itself is approximately eight minutes by car from [[Amagi Station]] on the [[Amagi Railway Amagi Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>

{{Short description|Kofun period burial mound in Asakura, Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Oda Chausutsuka Kofun | native_name =小田茶臼塚古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = | image_size = | alt = | caption = | map_type = Japan Fukuoka Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Oda Chausutsuka Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|33|23|18|N|130|39|53|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Asakura, Fukuoka]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Kyushu]] | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.5th century | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes ={{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|33|23|18|N|130|39|53|E}}}}}}
The {{nihongo|'''Oda Chausutsuka Kofun'''|小田茶臼塚古墳}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[zenpokoenfun|burial mound]], located in the Oda neighborhood of former [[Amagi, Fukuoka|Amagi]] city in what is now part of the city of [[Asakura, Fukuoka|Asakura]], [[Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1979.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/db/heritages/detail/202896|title=小田茶臼塚古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=December 20, 2023}}</ref> It is believed to have been built in the late 5th century.
==Overview== The Oda Chausutsuka Kofun is located on the eastern edge of the Chikugo Plain, on the right bank of the Sada River, a tributary of the [[Chikugo River]]. Its existence has been known for a long time, and it is listed in a local history texts on [[Chikuzen Province]] in 1798 and 1863. The total length of the tumulus is 54.5 meters, and it is a {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. The diameter of the posterior circular portion is 39.7 meters, and the width of the rectangular anterior portion is 25 meters, narrowing to 14.5 meter at the construction between the two portions. The length of this anterior portion is only 14.5 meters, which is short for a typical tumulus of this style. The height of the tumulus os 5 meters. The tumulus is orientated to the north, but the opening for the horizontal passage-style stone [[burial chamber]] is to the southwest. This chamber measures 3.5 meters long by 2.2 meters wide and 1.6 meters high. The tumulus was surrounded by a moat 4 meters wide and 0.6 meters deep. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The posterior circular part was constructed in three stages, with the bottom stage being carved out of the ground, and the second and higher stages being mounds with ''[[fukiishi]]''. The anterior part was built in two stages, and in the second stage a row of cylindrical and "morning-glory shaped" ''[[haniwa]]'' was detected. Additionally, a large [[Sue ware]] jar and a utensil stand were placed above the constriction.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
During the construction of a road in 1928, the southeastern portion of the posterior circular part was destroyed and the burial chamber exposed. A local teacher from Asakura Junior High School (currently Asakura High School) conducted a survey, and many of the excavated [[grave goods]] are still stored at the school. A formal [[archaeological excavation]] was not conducted until 1973. The grave goods recovered in 1928 included a short armor with horizontal strips, a helmet, a [[pauldron]], an iron sword, two iron spears, five iron bars, a bundle of iron arrowheads, eight cyliindrical beads, 226 round glass beads, and 161 small beads. Also found were horse harnesses, a four-ringed bell, stone pestle and ancient Sue ware pottery.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
Approximately three kilometers north of the tumulus is the Amagi Historical Museum, which displays excavated items. The tumulus itself is approximately eight minutes by car from [[Amagi Station]] on the [[Amagi Railway Amagi Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>

The {{nihongo|'''Oda Chausutsuka Kofun'''|小田茶臼塚古墳}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[zenpokoenfun|Kofun]], located in the Oda neighborhood of former [[Amagi, Fukuoka|Amagi]] city in what is now part of the city of [[Asakura, Fukuoka|Asakura]], [[Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1979.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/db/heritages/detail/202896|title=小田茶臼塚古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=December 20, 2023}}</ref> It is believed to have been built in the late 5th century.
==Overview== The Oda Chausutsuka Kofun is located on the eastern edge of the Chikugo Plain, on the right bank of the Sada River, a tributary of the [[Chikugo River]]. Its existence has been known for a long time, and it is listed in a local history texts on [[Chikuzen Province]] in 1798 and 1863. The total length of the tumulus is 54.5 meters, and it is a {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. The diameter of the posterior circular portion is 39.7 meters, and the width of the rectangular anterior portion is 25 meters, narrowing to 14.5 meter at the construction between the two portions. The length of this anterior portion is only 14.5 meters, which is short for a typical tumulus of this style. The height of the tumulus os 5 meters. The tumulus is orientated to the north, but the opening for the horizontal passage-style stone [[burial chamber]] is to the southwest. This chamber measures 3.5 meters long by 2.2 meters wide and 1.6 meters high. The tumulus was surrounded by a moat 4 meters wide and 0.6 meters deep. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The posterior circular part was constructed in three stages, with the bottom stage being carved out of the ground, and the second and higher stages being mounds with ''[[fukiishi]]''. The anterior part was built in two stages, and in the second stage a row of cylindrical and "morning-glory shaped" ''[[haniwa]]'' was detected. Additionally, a large [[Sue pottery]] jar and a utensil stand were placed above the constriction.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
During the construction of a road in 1928, the southeastern portion of the posterior circular part was destroyed and the burial chamber exposed. A local teacher from Asakura Junior High School (currently Asakura High School) conducted a survey, and many of the excavated [[grave goods]] are still stored at the school. A formal [[archaeological excavation]] was not conducted until 1973. The grave goods recovered in 1928 included a short armor with horizontal strips, a helmet, a [[pauldron]], an iron sword, two iron spears, five iron bars, a bundle of iron arrowheads, eight cyliindrical beads, 226 round glass beads, and 161 small beads. Also found were horse harnesses, a four-ringed bell, stone pestle and ancient Sue pottery pottery.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
Approximately three kilometers north of the tumulus is the Amagi Historical Museum, which displays excavated items. The tumulus itself is approximately eight minutes by car from [[Amagi Station]] on the [[Amagi Railway Amagi Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Fukuoka)]]
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== *[https://www.city.asakura.lg.jp/www/contents/1297732321710/index.html Asakura city home page] {{in lang|ja}}

The {{nihongo|'''Oda Chausutsuka Kofun'''|小田茶臼塚古墳}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[zenpokoenfun|Kofun]], located in the Oda neighborhood of former [[Amagi, Fukuoka|Amagi]] city in what is now part of the city of [[Asakura, Fukuoka|Asakura]], [[Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1979.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/db/heritages/detail/202896|title=小田茶臼塚古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=December 20, 2023}}</ref> It is believed to have been built in the late 5th century.
==Overview== The Oda Chausutsuka Kofun is located on the eastern edge of the Chikugo Plain, on the right bank of the Sada River, a tributary of the [[Chikugo River]]. Its existence has been known for a long time, and it is listed in a local history texts on [[Chikuzen Province]] in 1798 and 1863. The total length of the tumulus is 54.5 meters, and it is a {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. The diameter of the posterior circular portion is 39.7 meters, and the width of the rectangular anterior portion is 25 meters, narrowing to 14.5 meter at the construction between the two portions. The length of this anterior portion is only 14.5 meters, which is short for a typical tumulus of this style. The height of the tumulus os 5 meters. The tumulus is orientated to the north, but the opening for the horizontal passage-style stone [[burial chamber]] is to the southwest. This chamber measures 3.5 meters long by 2.2 meters wide and 1.6 meters high. The tumulus was surrounded by a moat 4 meters wide and 0.6 meters deep. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The posterior circular part was constructed in three stages, with the bottom stage being carved out of the ground, and the second and higher stages being mounds with ''[[fukiishi]]''. The anterior part was built in two stages, and in the second stage a row of cylindrical and "morning-glory shaped" ''[[haniwa]]'' was detected. Additionally, a large [[Sue pottery]] jar and a utensil stand were placed above the constriction.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
During the construction of a road in 1928, the southeastern portion of the posterior circular part was destroyed and the burial chamber exposed. A local teacher from Asakura Junior High School (currently Asakura High School) conducted a survey, and many of the excavated [[grave goods]] are still stored at the school. A formal [[archaeological excavation]] was not conducted until 1973. The grave goods recovered in 1928 included a short armor with horizontal strips, a helmet, a [[pauldron]], an iron sword, two iron spears, five iron bars, a bundle of iron arrowheads, eight cyliindrical beads, 226 round glass beads, and 161 small beads. Also found were horse harnesses, a four-ringed bell, stone pestle and ancient Sue pottery pottery.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
Approximately three kilometers north of the tumulus is the Amagi Historical Museum, which displays excavated items. The tumulus itself is approximately eight minutes by car from [[Amagi Station]] on the [[Amagi Railway Amagi Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Fukuoka)]]
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== *[https://www.city.asakura.lg.jp/www/contents/1297732321710/index.html Asakura city home page] {{in lang|ja}}

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Oda Chausutsuka Kofun]][[ja:小田茶臼塚古墳]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q17228007]] Wikidata name: Oda Chausutsuka Kofun [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:43, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Short description|Kofun period Kofun in Asakura, Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] | name = Oda Chausutsuka Kofun | native_name =小田茶臼塚古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = | image_size = | alt = | caption = | map_type = Japan Fukuoka Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Oda Chausutsuka Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{Coord|33.38834817|N|130.66483419|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_dot_label = | location = {{ill|Asakura|qid=Q720625}} | region = [[Kyushu]] | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.5th century | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes ={{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|33|23|18|N|130|39|53|E}}}}}}
The {{nihongo|'''Oda Chausutsuka Kofun'''|小田茶臼塚古墳}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Kofun]], located in the Oda neighborhood of former [[:en:Amagi, Fukuoka|Amagi]] city in what is now part of the city of [[:en:Asakura, Fukuoka|Asakura]], [[:en:Fukuoka Prefecture|Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1979.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/db/heritages/detail/202896|title=小田茶臼塚古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=December 20, 2023}}</ref> It is believed to have been built in the late 5th century.
==Overview== The Oda Chausutsuka Kofun is located on the eastern edge of the Chikugo Plain, on the right bank of the Sada River, a tributary of the [[:en:Chikugo River|Chikugo River]]. Its existence has been known for a long time, and it is listed in a local history texts on [[:en:Chikuzen Province|Chikuzen Province]] in 1798 and 1863. The total length of the tumulus is 54.5 meters, and it is a {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|comement=second one attempting to fix this error|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. The diameter of the posterior circular portion is 39.7 meters, and the width of the rectangular anterior portion is 25 meters, narrowing to 14.5 meter at the construction between the two portions. The length of this anterior portion is only 14.5 meters, which is short for a typical tumulus of this style. The height of the tumulus os 5 meters. The tumulus is orientated to the north, but the opening for the horizontal passage-style stone [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] is to the southwest. This chamber measures 3.5 meters long by 2.2 meters wide and 1.6 meters high. The tumulus was surrounded by a moat 4 meters wide and 0.6 meters deep. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The posterior circular part was constructed in three stages, with the bottom stage being carved out of the ground, and the second and higher stages being mounds with ''[[fukiishi]]''. The anterior part was built in two stages, and in the second stage a row of cylindrical and "morning-glory shaped" ''[[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]]'' was detected. Additionally, a large [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]] jar and a utensil stand were placed above the constriction.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
During the construction of a road in 1928, the southeastern portion of the posterior circular part was destroyed and the burial chamber exposed. A local teacher from Asakura Junior High School (currently Asakura High School) conducted a survey, and many of the excavated [[:en:Grave goods|Grave goods]] are still stored at the school. A formal [[archaeological excavation]] was not conducted until 1973. The grave goods recovered in 1928 included a short armor with horizontal strips, a helmet, a [[:en:pauldron|pauldron]], an iron sword, two iron spears, five iron bars, a bundle of iron arrowheads, eight cyliindrical beads, 226 round glass beads, and 161 small beads. Also found were horse harnesses, a four-ringed bell, stone pestle and ancient Sue pottery pottery.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
Approximately three kilometers north of the tumulus is the Amagi Historical Museum, which displays excavated items. The tumulus itself is approximately eight minutes by car from [[:en:Amagi Station|Amagi Station]] on the [[:en:Amagi Railway Amagi Line|Amagi Railway Amagi Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== *[[:en:List of Historic Sites of Japan (Fukuoka)|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Fukuoka)]]
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== *[https://www.city.asakura.lg.jp/www/contents/1297732321710/index.html Asakura city home page] {{in lang|ja}}

The posterior circular part was constructed in three stages, with the bottom stage being carved out of the ground, and the second and higher stages being mounds with ''[[fukiishi]]''. The anterior part was built in two stages, and in the second stage a row of cylindrical and "morning-glory shaped" ''[[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]]'' was detected. Additionally, a large [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]] jar and a utensil stand were placed above the constriction.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
During the construction of a road in 1928, the southeastern portion of the posterior circular part was destroyed and the burial chamber exposed. A local teacher from Asakura Junior High School (currently Asakura High School) conducted a survey, and many of the excavated [[:en:Grave goods|Grave goods]] are still stored at the school. A formal [[archaeological excavation]] was not conducted until 1973. The grave goods recovered in 1928 included a short armor with horizontal strips, a helmet, a [[:en:pauldron|pauldron]], an iron sword, two iron spears, five iron bars, a bundle of iron arrowheads, eight cyliindrical beads, 226 round glass beads, and 161 small beads. Also found were horse harnesses, a four-ringed bell, stone pestle and ancient Sue pottery pottery.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
Approximately three kilometers north of the tumulus is the Amagi Historical Museum, which displays excavated items. The tumulus itself is approximately eight minutes by car from [[:en:Amagi Station|Amagi Station]] on the [[:en:Amagi Railway Amagi Line|Amagi Railway Amagi Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>

The {{nihongo|'''Oda Chausutsuka Kofun'''|小田茶臼塚古墳}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Kofun]], located in the Oda neighborhood of former [[:en:Amagi, Fukuoka|Amagi]] city in what is now part of the city of [[:en:Asakura, Fukuoka|Asakura]], [[:en:Fukuoka Prefecture|Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1979.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/db/heritages/detail/202896|title=小田茶臼塚古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=December 20, 2023}}</ref> It is believed to have been built in the late 5th century.
==Overview== The Oda Chausutsuka Kofun is located on the eastern edge of the Chikugo Plain, on the right bank of the Sada River, a tributary of the [[:en:Chikugo River|Chikugo River]]. Its existence has been known for a long time, and it is listed in a local history texts on [[:en:Chikuzen Province|Chikuzen Province]] in 1798 and 1863. The total length of the tumulus is 54.5 meters, and it is a {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|comement=second one attempting to fix this error|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. The diameter of the posterior circular portion is 39.7 meters, and the width of the rectangular anterior portion is 25 meters, narrowing to 14.5 meter at the construction between the two portions. The length of this anterior portion is only 14.5 meters, which is short for a typical tumulus of this style. The height of the tumulus os 5 meters. The tumulus is orientated to the north, but the opening for the horizontal passage-style stone [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] is to the southwest. This chamber measures 3.5 meters long by 2.2 meters wide and 1.6 meters high. The tumulus was surrounded by a moat 4 meters wide and 0.6 meters deep. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The posterior circular part was constructed in three stages, with the bottom stage being carved out of the ground, and the second and higher stages being mounds with ''[[fukiishi]]''. The anterior part was built in two stages, and in the second stage a row of cylindrical and "morning-glory shaped" ''[[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]]'' was detected. Additionally, a large [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]] jar and a utensil stand were placed above the constriction.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
During the construction of a road in 1928, the southeastern portion of the posterior circular part was destroyed and the burial chamber exposed. A local teacher from Asakura Junior High School (currently Asakura High School) conducted a survey, and many of the excavated [[:en:Grave goods|Grave goods]] are still stored at the school. A formal [[archaeological excavation]] was not conducted until 1973. The grave goods recovered in 1928 included a short armor with horizontal strips, a helmet, a [[:en:pauldron|pauldron]], an iron sword, two iron spears, five iron bars, a bundle of iron arrowheads, eight cyliindrical beads, 226 round glass beads, and 161 small beads. Also found were horse harnesses, a four-ringed bell, stone pestle and ancient Sue pottery pottery.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
Approximately three kilometers north of the tumulus is the Amagi Historical Museum, which displays excavated items. The tumulus itself is approximately eight minutes by car from [[:en:Amagi Station|Amagi Station]] on the [[:en:Amagi Railway Amagi Line|Amagi Railway Amagi Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>

The length of this anterior portion is only 14.5 meters, which is short for a typical tumulus of this style. The height of the tumulus os 5 meters. The tumulus is orientated to the north, but the opening for the horizontal passage-style stone [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] is to the southwest. This chamber measures 3.5 meters long by 2.2 meters wide and 1.6 meters high. The tumulus was surrounded by a moat 4 meters wide and 0.6 meters deep. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The posterior circular part was constructed in three stages, with the bottom stage being carved out of the ground, and the second and higher stages being mounds with ''[[fukiishi]]''. The anterior part was built in two stages, and in the second stage a row of cylindrical and "morning-glory shaped" ''[[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]]'' was detected. Additionally, a large [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]] jar and a utensil stand were placed above the constriction.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
During the construction of a road in 1928, the southeastern portion of the posterior circular part was destroyed and the burial chamber exposed. A local teacher from Asakura Junior High School (currently Asakura High School) conducted a survey, and many of the excavated [[:en:Grave goods|Grave goods]] are still stored at the school. A formal [[archaeological excavation]] was not conducted until 1973. The grave goods recovered in 1928 included a short armor with horizontal strips, a helmet, a [[:en:pauldron|pauldron]], an iron sword, two iron spears, five iron bars, a bundle of iron arrowheads, eight cyliindrical beads, 226 round glass beads, and 161 small beads. Also found were horse harnesses, a four-ringed bell, stone pestle and ancient Sue pottery pottery.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
Approximately three kilometers north of the tumulus is the Amagi Historical Museum, which displays excavated items. The tumulus itself is approximately eight minutes by car from [[:en:Amagi Station|Amagi Station]] on the [[:en:Amagi Railway Amagi Line|Amagi Railway Amagi Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>

The {{nihongo|'''Oda Chausutsuka Kofun'''|小田茶臼塚古墳}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Kofun]], located in the Oda neighborhood of former [[:en:Amagi, Fukuoka|Amagi]] city in what is now part of the city of [[:en:Asakura, Fukuoka|Asakura]], [[:en:Fukuoka Prefecture|Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1979.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/db/heritages/detail/202896|title=小田茶臼塚古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=December 20, 2023}}</ref> It is believed to have been built in the late 5th century.
==Overview== The Oda Chausutsuka Kofun is located on the eastern edge of the Chikugo Plain, on the right bank of the Sada River, a tributary of the [[:en:Chikugo River|Chikugo River]]. Its existence has been known for a long time, and it is listed in a local history texts on [[:en:Chikuzen Province|Chikuzen Province]] in 1798 and 1863. The diameter of the posterior circular portion is 39.7 meters, and the width of the rectangular anterior portion is 25 meters, narrowing to 14.5 meter at the construction between the two portions. The length of this anterior portion is only 14.5 meters, which is short for a typical tumulus of this style. The height of the tumulus os 5 meters. The tumulus is orientated to the north, but the opening for the horizontal passage-style stone [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] is to the southwest. This chamber measures 3.5 meters long by 2.2 meters wide and 1.6 meters high. The tumulus was surrounded by a moat 4 meters wide and 0.6 meters deep. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>

The anterior part was built in two stages, and in the second stage a row of cylindrical and "morning-glory shaped" ''[[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]]'' was detected. Additionally, a large [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]] jar and a utensil stand were placed above the constriction.<ref name="Shiseki"/>

A local teacher from Asakura Junior High School (currently Asakura High School) conducted a survey, and many of the excavated [[:en:Grave goods|Grave goods]] are still stored at the school. A formal [[archaeological excavation]] was not conducted until 1973. The grave goods recovered in 1928 included a short armor with horizontal strips, a helmet, a [[:en:pauldron|pauldron]], an iron sword, two iron spears, five iron bars, a bundle of iron arrowheads, eight cyliindrical beads, 226 round glass beads, and 161 small beads. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1979.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/db/heritages/detail/202896|title=小田茶臼塚古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=December 20, 2023}}</ref> It is believed to have been built in the late 5th century.
==Overview== The Oda Chausutsuka Kofun is located on the eastern edge of the Chikugo Plain, on the right bank of the Sada River, a tributary of the [[:en:Chikugo River|Chikugo River]]. Its existence has been known for a long time, and it is listed in a local history texts on [[:en:Chikuzen Province|Chikuzen Province]] in 1798 and 1863. The total length of the tumulus is 54.5 meters, and it is a {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|comement=second one attempting to fix this error|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. The diameter of the posterior circular portion is 39.7 meters, and the width of the rectangular anterior portion is 25 meters, narrowing to 14.5 meter at the construction between the two portions. The length of this anterior portion is only 14.5 meters, which is short for a typical tumulus of this style. The height of the tumulus os 5 meters. The tumulus is orientated to the north, but the opening for the horizontal passage-style stone [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] is to the southwest. This chamber measures 3.5 meters long by 2.2 meters wide and 1.6 meters high. The tumulus was surrounded by a moat 4 meters wide and 0.6 meters deep. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The posterior circular part was constructed in three stages, with the bottom stage being carved out of the ground, and the second and higher stages being mounds with ''[[fukiishi]]''. The anterior part was built in two stages, and in the second stage a row of cylindrical and "morning-glory shaped" ''[[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]]'' was detected. Additionally, a large [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]] jar and a utensil stand were placed above the constriction.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
During the construction of a road in 1928, the southeastern portion of the posterior circular part was destroyed and the burial chamber exposed. A local teacher from Asakura Junior High School (currently Asakura High School) conducted a survey, and many of the excavated [[:en:Grave goods|Grave goods]] are still stored at the school. A formal [[archaeological excavation]] was not conducted until 1973. The grave goods recovered in 1928 included a short armor with horizontal strips, a helmet, a [[:en:pauldron|pauldron]], an iron sword, two iron spears, five iron bars, a bundle of iron arrowheads, eight cyliindrical beads, 226 round glass beads, and 161 small beads. Also found were horse harnesses, a four-ringed bell, stone pestle and ancient Sue pottery pottery.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
Approximately three kilometers north of the tumulus is the Amagi Historical Museum, which displays excavated items. The tumulus itself is approximately eight minutes by car from [[:en:Amagi Station|Amagi Station]] on the [[:en:Amagi Railway Amagi Line|Amagi Railway Amagi Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== *[[:en:List of Historic Sites of Japan (Fukuoka)|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Fukuoka)]]

The {{nihongo|'''Oda Chausutsuka Kofun'''|小田茶臼塚古墳}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Kofun]], located in the Oda neighborhood of former [[:en:Amagi, Fukuoka|Amagi]] city in what is now part of the city of [[:en:Asakura, Fukuoka|Asakura]], [[:en:Fukuoka Prefecture|Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1979.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/db/heritages/detail/202896|title=小田茶臼塚古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=December 20, 2023}}</ref> It is believed to have been built in the late 5th century.
==Overview== The Oda Chausutsuka Kofun is located on the eastern edge of the Chikugo Plain, on the right bank of the Sada River, a tributary of the [[:en:Chikugo River|Chikugo River]]. Its existence has been known for a long time, and it is listed in a local history texts on [[:en:Chikuzen Province|Chikuzen Province]] in 1798 and 1863. The total length of the tumulus is 54.5 meters, and it is a {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|comement=second one attempting to fix this error|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. The diameter of the posterior circular portion is 39.7 meters, and the width of the rectangular anterior portion is 25 meters, narrowing to 14.5 meter at the construction between the two portions. The length of this anterior portion is only 14.5 meters, which is short for a typical tumulus of this style. The height of the tumulus os 5 meters. The tumulus is orientated to the north, but the opening for the horizontal passage-style stone [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] is to the southwest. This chamber measures 3.5 meters long by 2.2 meters wide and 1.6 meters high. The tumulus was surrounded by a moat 4 meters wide and 0.6 meters deep. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The posterior circular part was constructed in three stages, with the bottom stage being carved out of the ground, and the second and higher stages being mounds with ''[[fukiishi]]''. The anterior part was built in two stages, and in the second stage a row of cylindrical and "morning-glory shaped" ''[[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]]'' was detected. Additionally, a large [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]] jar and a utensil stand were placed above the constriction.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
During the construction of a road in 1928, the southeastern portion of the posterior circular part was destroyed and the burial chamber exposed. A local teacher from Asakura Junior High School (currently Asakura High School) conducted a survey, and many of the excavated [[:en:Grave goods|Grave goods]] are still stored at the school. A formal [[archaeological excavation]] was not conducted until 1973. The grave goods recovered in 1928 included a short armor with horizontal strips, a helmet, a [[:en:pauldron|pauldron]], an iron sword, two iron spears, five iron bars, a bundle of iron arrowheads, eight cyliindrical beads, 226 round glass beads, and 161 small beads. Also found were horse harnesses, a four-ringed bell, stone pestle and ancient Sue pottery pottery.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
Approximately three kilometers north of the tumulus is the Amagi Historical Museum, which displays excavated items. The tumulus itself is approximately eight minutes by car from [[:en:Amagi Station|Amagi Station]] on the [[:en:Amagi Railway Amagi Line|Amagi Railway Amagi Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== *[[:en:List of Historic Sites of Japan (Fukuoka)|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Fukuoka)]]

The tumulus was surrounded by a moat 4 meters wide and 0.6 meters deep. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The posterior circular part was constructed in three stages, with the bottom stage being carved out of the ground, and the second and higher stages being mounds with ''[[fukiishi]]''. The anterior part was built in two stages, and in the second stage a row of cylindrical and "morning-glory shaped" ''[[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]]'' was detected. Additionally, a large [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]] jar and a utensil stand were placed above the constriction.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
During the construction of a road in 1928, the southeastern portion of the posterior circular part was destroyed and the burial chamber exposed. A local teacher from Asakura Junior High School (currently Asakura High School) conducted a survey, and many of the excavated [[:en:Grave goods|Grave goods]] are still stored at the school. A formal [[archaeological excavation]] was not conducted until 1973. The grave goods recovered in 1928 included a short armor with horizontal strips, a helmet, a [[:en:pauldron|pauldron]], an iron sword, two iron spears, five iron bars, a bundle of iron arrowheads, eight cyliindrical beads, 226 round glass beads, and 161 small beads. Also found were horse harnesses, a four-ringed bell, stone pestle and ancient Sue pottery pottery.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
Approximately three kilometers north of the tumulus is the Amagi Historical Museum, which displays excavated items. The tumulus itself is approximately eight minutes by car from [[:en:Amagi Station|Amagi Station]] on the [[:en:Amagi Railway Amagi Line|Amagi Railway Amagi Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== *[[:en:List of Historic Sites of Japan (Fukuoka)|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Fukuoka)]]

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Oda Chausutsuka Kofun]][[ja:小田茶臼塚古墳]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Oda Chausutsuka Kofun]][[ja:小田茶臼塚古墳]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Oda Chausutsuka Kofun]][[ja:小田茶臼塚古墳]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q17228007]] Wikidata name: Oda Chausutsuka Kofun [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:43, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Short description|Kofun period Kofun in Asakura, Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|en|Zenpokoenfun|WD=Q11268718|lt=Zenpokoenfun}} | name = Oda Chausutsuka Kofun | native_name =小田茶臼塚古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = | image_size = | alt = | caption = | map_type = Japan Fukuoka Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Oda Chausutsuka Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{Coord|33.38834817|N|130.66483419|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_dot_label = | location = {{ill|Asakura|WD=Q720625}} | region = [[Kyushu]] | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.5th century | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes ={{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|33|23|18|N|130|39|53|E}}}}}}
The {{nihongo|'''Oda Chausutsuka Kofun'''|小田茶臼塚古墳}} is a [[Kofun period]] {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|en|Zenpokoenfun|WD=Q11268718|lt=Kofun}}, located in the Oda neighborhood of former {{ill|Amagi, Fukuoka|en|Amagi, Fukuoka|WD=Q1208957|lt=Amagi}} city in what is now part of the city of {{ill|Asakura, Fukuoka|en|Asakura, Fukuoka|WD=Q720625|lt=Asakura}}, {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|en|Fukuoka Prefecture|WD=Q123258|lt=Fukuoka Prefecture}} {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan}}. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1979.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/db/heritages/detail/202896|title=小田茶臼塚古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=December 20, 2023}}</ref> It is believed to have been built in the late 5th century.
==Overview== The Oda Chausutsuka Kofun is located on the eastern edge of the Chikugo Plain, on the right bank of the Sada River, a tributary of the {{ill|Chikugo River|en|Chikugo River|WD=Q954320|lt=Chikugo River}}. Its existence has been known for a long time, and it is listed in a local history texts on {{ill|Chikuzen Province|en|Chikuzen Province|WD=Q1047664|lt=Chikuzen Province}} in 1798 and 1863. The total length of the tumulus is 54.5 meters, and it is a {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|comement=second one attempting to fix this error|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. The diameter of the posterior circular portion is 39.7 meters, and the width of the rectangular anterior portion is 25 meters, narrowing to 14.5 meter at the construction between the two portions. The length of this anterior portion is only 14.5 meters, which is short for a typical tumulus of this style. The height of the tumulus os 5 meters. The tumulus is orientated to the north, but the opening for the horizontal passage-style stone [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] is to the southwest. This chamber measures 3.5 meters long by 2.2 meters wide and 1.6 meters high. The tumulus was surrounded by a moat 4 meters wide and 0.6 meters deep. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The posterior circular part was constructed in three stages, with the bottom stage being carved out of the ground, and the second and higher stages being mounds with ''[[fukiishi]]''. The anterior part was built in two stages, and in the second stage a row of cylindrical and "morning-glory shaped" ''{{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|WD=Q849376|lt=Haniwa}}'' was detected. Additionally, a large {{ill|Sue pottery|en|Sue pottery|WD=Q7634351|lt=Sue pottery}} jar and a utensil stand were placed above the constriction.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
During the construction of a road in 1928, the southeastern portion of the posterior circular part was destroyed and the burial chamber exposed. A local teacher from Asakura Junior High School (currently Asakura High School) conducted a survey, and many of the excavated {{ill|Grave goods|en|Grave goods|WD=Q1399692|lt=Grave goods}} are still stored at the school. A formal [[archaeological excavation]] was not conducted until 1973. The grave goods recovered in 1928 included a short armor with horizontal strips, a helmet, a {{ill|pauldron|en|pauldron|WD=Q340673|lt=pauldron}}, an iron sword, two iron spears, five iron bars, a bundle of iron arrowheads, eight cyliindrical beads, 226 round glass beads, and 161 small beads. Also found were horse harnesses, a four-ringed bell, stone pestle and ancient Sue pottery pottery.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
Approximately three kilometers north of the tumulus is the Amagi Historical Museum, which displays excavated items. The tumulus itself is approximately eight minutes by car from {{ill|Amagi Station|en|Amagi Station|WD=Q4739135|lt=Amagi Station}} on the {{ill|Amagi Railway Amagi Line|en|Amagi Railway Amagi Line|WD=Q642407|lt=Amagi Railway Amagi Line}}.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== *{{ill|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Fukuoka)|en|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Fukuoka)|WD=Q13641288|lt=List of Historic Sites of Japan (Fukuoka)}}

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Oda Chausutsuka Kofun]][[ja:小田茶臼塚古墳]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Oda Chausutsuka Kofun]][[ja:小田茶臼塚古墳]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Oda Chausutsuka Kofun]][[ja:小田茶臼塚古墳]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q17228007]] Wikidata name: Oda Chausutsuka Kofun [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:43, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Short description|Kofun period Kofun in Asakura, Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|en|Zenpokoenfun|WD=Q11268718|lt=Zenpokoenfun}} | name = Oda Chausutsuka Kofun | native_name =小田茶臼塚古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = | image_size = | alt = | caption = | map_type = Japan Fukuoka Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Oda Chausutsuka Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{Coord|33.38834817|N|130.66483419|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_dot_label = | location = {{ill|Asakura|WD=Q720625}} | region = [[Kyushu]] | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.5th century | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes ={{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|33|23|18|N|130|39|53|E}}}}}}
The {{nihongo|'''Oda Chausutsuka Kofun'''|小田茶臼塚古墳}} is a [[Kofun period]] {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|en|Zenpokoenfun|WD=Q11268718|lt=Kofun}}, located in the Oda neighborhood of former {{ill|Amagi, Fukuoka|en|Amagi, Fukuoka|WD=Q1208957|lt=Amagi}} city in what is now part of the city of {{ill|Asakura, Fukuoka|en|Asakura, Fukuoka|WD=Q720625|lt=Asakura}}, {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|en|Fukuoka Prefecture|WD=Q123258|lt=Fukuoka Prefecture}} {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan}}. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1979.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/db/heritages/detail/202896|title=小田茶臼塚古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=December 20, 2023}}</ref> It is believed to have been built in the late 5th century.
==Overview== The Oda Chausutsuka Kofun is located on the eastern edge of the Chikugo Plain, on the right bank of the Sada River, a tributary of the {{ill|Chikugo River|en|Chikugo River|WD=Q954320|lt=Chikugo River}}. Its existence has been known for a long time, and it is listed in a local history texts on {{ill|Chikuzen Province|en|Chikuzen Province|WD=Q1047664|lt=Chikuzen Province}} in 1798 and 1863. The total length of the tumulus is 54.5 meters, and it is a {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|comement=second one attempting to fix this error|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. The diameter of the posterior circular portion is 39.7 meters, and the width of the rectangular anterior portion is 25 meters, narrowing to 14.5 meter at the construction between the two portions. The length of this anterior portion is only 14.5 meters, which is short for a typical tumulus of this style. The height of the tumulus os 5 meters. The tumulus is orientated to the north, but the opening for the horizontal passage-style stone [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] is to the southwest. This chamber measures 3.5 meters long by 2.2 meters wide and 1.6 meters high. The tumulus was surrounded by a moat 4 meters wide and 0.6 meters deep. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The posterior circular part was constructed in three stages, with the bottom stage being carved out of the ground, and the second and higher stages being mounds with ''[[fukiishi]]''. The anterior part was built in two stages, and in the second stage a row of cylindrical and "morning-glory shaped" ''{{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|WD=Q849376|lt=Haniwa}}'' was detected. Additionally, a large {{ill|Sue pottery|en|Sue pottery|WD=Q7634351|lt=Sue pottery}} jar and a utensil stand were placed above the constriction.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
During the construction of a road in 1928, the southeastern portion of the posterior circular part was destroyed and the burial chamber exposed. A local teacher from Asakura Junior High School (currently Asakura High School) conducted a survey, and many of the excavated {{ill|Grave goods|en|Grave goods|WD=Q1399692|lt=Grave goods}} are still stored at the school. A formal [[archaeological excavation]] was not conducted until 1973. The grave goods recovered in 1928 included a short armor with horizontal strips, a helmet, a {{ill|pauldron|en|pauldron|WD=Q340673|lt=pauldron}}, an iron sword, two iron spears, five iron bars, a bundle of iron arrowheads, eight cyliindrical beads, 226 round glass beads, and 161 small beads. Also found were horse harnesses, a four-ringed bell, stone pestle and ancient Sue pottery pottery.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
Approximately three kilometers north of the tumulus is the Amagi Historical Museum, which displays excavated items. The tumulus itself is approximately eight minutes by car from {{ill|Amagi Station|en|Amagi Station|WD=Q4739135|lt=Amagi Station}} on the {{ill|Amagi Railway Amagi Line|en|Amagi Railway Amagi Line|WD=Q642407|lt=Amagi Railway Amagi Line}}.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== *{{ill|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Fukuoka)|en|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Fukuoka)|WD=Q13641288|lt=List of Historic Sites of Japan (Fukuoka)}}
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== *[https://www.city.asakura.lg.jp/www/contents/1297732321710/index.html Asakura city home page] {{in lang|ja}}
{{Kofun navbar}}

Its existence has been known for a long time, and it is listed in a local history texts on {{ill|Chikuzen Province|en|Chikuzen Province|WD=Q1047664|lt=Chikuzen Province}} in 1798 and 1863. The total length of the tumulus is 54.5 meters, and it is a {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|comement=second one attempting to fix this error|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. The diameter of the posterior circular portion is 39.7 meters, and the width of the rectangular anterior portion is 25 meters, narrowing to 14.5 meter at the construction between the two portions. The length of this anterior portion is only 14.5 meters, which is short for a typical tumulus of this style. The height of the tumulus os 5 meters. The tumulus is orientated to the north, but the opening for the horizontal passage-style stone [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] is to the southwest. This chamber measures 3.5 meters long by 2.2 meters wide and 1.6 meters high. The tumulus was surrounded by a moat 4 meters wide and 0.6 meters deep. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The posterior circular part was constructed in three stages, with the bottom stage being carved out of the ground, and the second and higher stages being mounds with ''[[fukiishi]]''. The anterior part was built in two stages, and in the second stage a row of cylindrical and "morning-glory shaped" ''{{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|WD=Q849376|lt=Haniwa}}'' was detected. Additionally, a large {{ill|Sue pottery|en|Sue pottery|WD=Q7634351|lt=Sue pottery}} jar and a utensil stand were placed above the constriction.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
During the construction of a road in 1928, the southeastern portion of the posterior circular part was destroyed and the burial chamber exposed. A local teacher from Asakura Junior High School (currently Asakura High School) conducted a survey, and many of the excavated {{ill|Grave goods|en|Grave goods|WD=Q1399692|lt=Grave goods}} are still stored at the school. A formal [[archaeological excavation]] was not conducted until 1973. The grave goods recovered in 1928 included a short armor with horizontal strips, a helmet, a {{ill|pauldron|en|pauldron|WD=Q340673|lt=pauldron}}, an iron sword, two iron spears, five iron bars, a bundle of iron arrowheads, eight cyliindrical beads, 226 round glass beads, and 161 small beads. Also found were horse harnesses, a four-ringed bell, stone pestle and ancient Sue pottery pottery.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
Approximately three kilometers north of the tumulus is the Amagi Historical Museum, which displays excavated items. The tumulus itself is approximately eight minutes by car from {{ill|Amagi Station|en|Amagi Station|WD=Q4739135|lt=Amagi Station}} on the {{ill|Amagi Railway Amagi Line|en|Amagi Railway Amagi Line|WD=Q642407|lt=Amagi Railway Amagi Line}}.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== *{{ill|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Fukuoka)|en|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Fukuoka)|WD=Q13641288|lt=List of Historic Sites of Japan (Fukuoka)}}

The grave goods recovered in 1928 included a short armor with horizontal strips, a helmet, a {{ill|pauldron|en|pauldron|WD=Q340673|lt=pauldron|1=Pauldron}}, an iron sword, two iron spears, five iron bars, a bundle of iron arrowheads, eight cyliindrical beads, 226 round glass beads, and 161 small beads. Also found were horse harnesses, a four-ringed bell, stone pestle and ancient Sue pottery pottery.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
Approximately three kilometers north of the tumulus is the Amagi Historical Museum, which displays excavated items. The tumulus itself is approximately eight minutes by car from {{ill|Amagi Station|en|Amagi Station|WD=Q4739135|lt=Amagi Station|1=Amagi Station}} on the {{ill|Amagi Railway Amagi Line|en|Amagi Railway Amagi Line|WD=Q642407|lt=Amagi Railway Amagi Line|1=Amagi Railway Amagi Line}}.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== *{{ill|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Fukuoka)|en|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Fukuoka)|WD=Q13641288|lt=List of Historic Sites of Japan (Fukuoka)|1=List of Historic Sites of Japan (Fukuoka)}}

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Oda Chausutsuka Kofun]][[ja:小田茶臼塚古墳]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Oda Chausutsuka Kofun]][[ja:小田茶臼塚古墳]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Oda Chausutsuka Kofun]][[ja:小田茶臼塚古墳]]
{{Short description|Kofun period Kofun in Asakura, Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|en|Zenpokoenfun|WD=Q11268718|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpōkōenfun}} | name = Oda Chausutsuka Kofun | native_name =小田茶臼塚古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = | image_size = | alt = | caption = | map_type = Japan Fukuoka Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Oda Chausutsuka Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{Coord|33.38834817|N|130.66483419|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_dot_label = | location = {{ill|Asakura|WD=Q720625|1=Asakura, Fukuoka}} | region = [[Kyushu]] | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.5th century | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes ={{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|33|23|18|N|130|39|53|E}}}}}}
The {{nihongo|'''Oda Chausutsuka Kofun'''|小田茶臼塚古墳}} is a [[Kofun period]] {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|en|Zenpokoenfun|WD=Q11268718|lt=Kofun|1=Zenpōkōenfun}}, located in the Oda neighborhood of former {{ill|Amagi, Fukuoka|en|Amagi, Fukuoka|WD=Q1208957|lt=Amagi|1=Amagi, Fukuoka}} city in what is now part of the city of {{ill|Asakura, Fukuoka|en|Asakura, Fukuoka|WD=Q720625|lt=Asakura|1=Asakura, Fukuoka}}, {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|en|Fukuoka Prefecture|WD=Q123258|lt=Fukuoka Prefecture|1=Fukuoka Prefecture}} {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}}. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1979.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/db/heritages/detail/202896|title=小田茶臼塚古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=December 20, 2023}}</ref> It is believed to have been built in the late 5th century.
==Overview== The Oda Chausutsuka Kofun is located on the eastern edge of the Chikugo Plain, on the right bank of the Sada River, a tributary of the {{ill|Chikugo River|en|Chikugo River|WD=Q954320|lt=Chikugo River|1=Chikugo River}}. Its existence has been known for a long time, and it is listed in a local history texts on {{ill|Chikuzen Province|en|Chikuzen Province|WD=Q1047664|lt=Chikuzen Province|1=Chikuzen Province}} in 1798 and 1863. The total length of the tumulus is 54.5 meters, and it is a {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|comement=second one attempting to fix this error|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. The diameter of the posterior circular portion is 39.7 meters, and the width of the rectangular anterior portion is 25 meters, narrowing to 14.5 meter at the construction between the two portions. The length of this anterior portion is only 14.5 meters, which is short for a typical tumulus of this style. The height of the tumulus os 5 meters. The tumulus is orientated to the north, but the opening for the horizontal passage-style stone [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] is to the southwest. This chamber measures 3.5 meters long by 2.2 meters wide and 1.6 meters high. The tumulus was surrounded by a moat 4 meters wide and 0.6 meters deep. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The posterior circular part was constructed in three stages, with the bottom stage being carved out of the ground, and the second and higher stages being mounds with ''[[fukiishi]]''. The anterior part was built in two stages, and in the second stage a row of cylindrical and "morning-glory shaped" ''{{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|WD=Q849376|lt=Haniwa|1=Haniwa}}'' was detected. Additionally, a large {{ill|Sue pottery|en|Sue pottery|WD=Q7634351|lt=Sue pottery|1=Sue pottery}} jar and a utensil stand were placed above the constriction.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
During the construction of a road in 1928, the southeastern portion of the posterior circular part was destroyed and the burial chamber exposed. A local teacher from Asakura Junior High School (currently Asakura High School) conducted a survey, and many of the excavated {{ill|Grave goods|en|Grave goods|WD=Q1399692|lt=Grave goods|1=Grave goods}} are still stored at the school. A formal [[archaeological excavation]] was not conducted until 1973. The grave goods recovered in 1928 included a short armor with horizontal strips, a helmet, a {{ill|pauldron|en|pauldron|WD=Q340673|lt=pauldron|1=Pauldron}}, an iron sword, two iron spears, five iron bars, a bundle of iron arrowheads, eight cyliindrical beads, 226 round glass beads, and 161 small beads. Also found were horse harnesses, a four-ringed bell, stone pestle and ancient Sue pottery pottery.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
Approximately three kilometers north of the tumulus is the Amagi Historical Museum, which displays excavated items. The tumulus itself is approximately eight minutes by car from {{ill|Amagi Station|en|Amagi Station|WD=Q4739135|lt=Amagi Station|1=Amagi Station}} on the {{ill|Amagi Railway Amagi Line|en|Amagi Railway Amagi Line|WD=Q642407|lt=Amagi Railway Amagi Line|1=Amagi Railway Amagi Line}}.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== *{{ill|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Fukuoka)|en|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Fukuoka)|WD=Q13641288|lt=List of Historic Sites of Japan (Fukuoka)|1=List of Historic Sites of Japan (Fukuoka)}}
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== *[https://www.city.asakura.lg.jp/www/contents/1297732321710/index.html Asakura city home page] {{in lang|ja}}
